region_of_origin: Singapore (fusion dish served at Indian-Mexican restaurants)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for paneer tikka fajitas; closest parent is the generic fajita food product class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Wheat flour tortilla-based dish (closest parent for fajita-type wraps)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for paneer tikka fajitas; A0FKP (flat bread / tortilla-based dish) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for paneer tikka fajitas or a sufficiently close parent concept.

main_ingredients: tikka spice marinade (cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, garam masala, yoghurt)

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of two paneer tikka fajita wraps, each filled with pan-seared spiced paneer, sautéed bell peppers, and onions in a wheat flour tortilla, totalling approximately 300 g.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: Paneer (ICMR-NIN, Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, 2017), Wheat flour tortilla (USDA FoodData Central, SR Legacy), Bell peppers and onions (USDA FoodData Central, SR Legacy), Tikka marinade spices (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for paneer tikka fajitas exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values estimated from weighted component analysis: approximately 35% paneer, 30% wheat tortilla, 20% mixed vegetables (bell peppers, onions), 10% tikka marinade (yoghurt, spices), 5% oil. ICMR-NIN 2017 used for paneer and spice components; USDA SR Legacy used for tortilla and vegetable components. Values represent a plausible per-100g composite.

gi_source.title: Wheat flour tortilla / flatbread GI analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for paneer tikka fajitas. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle being a wheat flour tortilla (GI ~52 per Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables; wheat tortilla entry). Paneer and vegetables contribute negligible glycemic load. The protein and fat content from paneer and oil further attenuate the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 68.4) / 100 ≈ 35.6, rounded to 35.8 accounting for fibre adjustment.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1260 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single serving of paneer tikka fajitas may contribute approximately 50–55% of the recommended daily sodium limit (2300 mg); portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~35.8 per serving) driven by wheat flour tortilla carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note the moderate glycemic load per serving (~35.8 GL units) from the wheat tortilla base; limiting to one wrap, choosing a high-fibre or whole-wheat tortilla variant, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and dairy marinade (~4.8 g per 100 g; ~14.4 g per serving) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, the saturated fat contribution from paneer (~14.4 g per serving) approaches or exceeds the recommended daily limit of 20 g; reducing portion size or substituting low-fat paneer and using minimal oil during searing is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Tikka Fajitas is an Indian-Mexican fusion dish served at specialty restaurants in Singapore, notably at Three Amigos Indian and Mexican Restaurant. The dish features cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) that are marinated in a tikka spice blend — typically comprising yoghurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, red chilli, and garam masala — then pan-seared or grilled until lightly charred and smoky. The spiced paneer is served alongside sautéed bell peppers and onions inside warm wheat flour tortillas, combining the bold aromatic flavours of North Indian tikka cuisine with the format of a Mexican fajita. The texture contrasts soft, slightly springy paneer with the crunch of fresh vegetables and the pliable chew of the tortilla. Nutritionally, each serving (approximately 300 g, two wraps) provides around 654 kcal, 27.6 g protein, 31.5 g fat, and 68.4 g carbohydrate. Paneer contributes significant calcium (~444 mg per serving) and complete protein. The dish is vegetarian and suitable for halal diets. Clinically, the moderate glycemic load (~35.8 per serving), elevated sodium (~1260 mg per serving), and saturated fat content (~14.4 g per serving) are relevant considerations for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is a culturally distinctive fusion item reflecting Singapore's multicultural food landscape.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for paneer tikka fajitas exists in any approved primary source (ICMR-NIN, HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA). Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component analysis using ICMR-NIN 2017 for paneer and spice components and USDA SR Legacy for tortilla and vegetable components. GI is estimated by analogy to wheat flour tortilla from the International Tables of Glycemic Index (Atkinson et al. 2008). Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved primary sources and the dish composition is well-defined.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component analysis; no direct approved-source entry exists for this fusion dish. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources (ICMR-NIN 2017 and USDA SR Legacy do not provide reliable vitamin D values for these ingredients in this preparation context). 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN 2017 for paneer or the spice components, and USDA SR Legacy values for the vegetable components are negligible and not reliably aggregated. 4. GI is an analogous estimate based on wheat flour tortilla as the dominant glycemic carbohydrate vehicle; the protein and fat from paneer attenuate the overall glycemic response. 5. The food_id GD000447 is sourced from field survey data seed data; the dish is served by two restaurants under the Three Amigos brand in Singapore. 6. The cuisine is classified as Indian-Mexican Fusion reflecting the hybrid nature of the dish; the input record listed 'Mexican' which is partially correct but does not capture the dominant Indian tikka preparation. 7. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given this is a Singapore-served dish. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from component analysis and carry medium confidence.
